title: pinus radiata subsp. cedrosensis
intro: Pinus radiata subsp. cedrosensis, also known as Cedros Island redwood, is a subspecies of the Monterey pine (Pinus radiata) that is native to Cedros Island off the coast of Baja California, Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and reduce the risk of infection. The health benefits of Pinus radiata subsp. cedrosensis include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Pinus radiata subsp. cedrosensis include essential oils, extracts, and teas, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
